question_text: Which of the following is an example of elastic deformation?

options.label: A
options.text: Stretching a rubber band and it returns to its original shape

options.label: B
options.text: Bending a metal ruler so it stays bent

options.label: C
options.text: Breaking a piece of chalk in half

options.label: D
options.text: Crumpling a piece of paper permanently

correct_answer: Stretching a rubber band and it returns to its original shape
explanation: Elastic deformation occurs when an object changes shape but returns to its original shape once the force is removed. A rubber band is an ideal example because it stretches when pulled and snaps back to its original length. In contrast, bending a ruler permanently (plastic deformation), breaking chalk, and crumpling paper are all examples of inelastic deformation, where the object does not return to its original shape.
